title: WeeGee

{{Quote|I could never have left Weeg behind on Sulon. Besides myself, he's the last surviving member of the Katarn family.|[[Kyle Katarn]]|<ref name="NEGD" />}}
'''WeeGee''', nicknamed '''Weeg''', was the [[Katarn family|Katarns']] multi-purpose droid, hand-built by [[Morgan Katarn]].

==History==
WeeGee was considered part of the [[Katarn family]]. Since Kyle was twelve, he started to maintain WeeGee himself, and knew all his circuits and servos.<ref name="RA" /> One time he saved young Kyle from drowning in a pool, and they agreed not to let his father know about it.<ref name="RAAD">[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]]</ref><ref name="RA" /> However Kyle parted from WeeGee when he enrolled at the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]] in [[5 BBY/Legends|5 BBY]].<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Characters">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref>

At some point around [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], he saw Morgan leaving for a trip, and returning changed from it. Soon after they hosted the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]], who entrusted to Morgan a book, and [[Qu Rahn's lightsaber|his lightsaber]] for his son. Morgan hid it inside a compartment of the droid, for whenever his son would return there and play a certain encrypted [[Hologram disc/Legends|holodisc]]. Following the Jedi's departure, WeeGee helped Morgan make work on the workshop's roof, installing tiles and engraving etchings on them; WeeGee didn't know that the patterns he so much appreciated artistically were actually a [[map to the Valley of the Jedi]]. After about a [[standard month/Legends|month]] of work, Morgan ordered the droid to cover the etchings with [[plaster]]; WeeGee never knew their significance and supposed that Morgan just didn't enjoy the designs.<ref name="RA" />

During the [[Subjugation of Sulon]] later that year, the [[Ghost Battalion]] stormed into the [[Katarn family compound]] looking for Morgan, who, however was out. As [[Koyo]] and [[Santo (soldier)|Santo]] opened the door to the workshop, WeeGee hurled a partially disassembled servo mechanism towards [[Brazack|their leader]]. The commandos threw an [[Anti-Personnel Grenade]], and Santo put a beam through WeeGee's [[Speaker/Legends|speaker]] grill to mute him. WeeGee considered against further resistance, and silently transmitted an electronic warning to Morgan.<ref name="SFTE">''[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire]]''</ref>

[[File:Kylekatarn_jasoncourt.jpg|thumb|WeeGee giving [[Qu Rahn's lightsaber]] to Kyle.]]
WeeGee remained out of commission and left dumped on the floor in the workshop, despite the house being ransacked several times. Some years later, in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], [[Imperial Remnant|Imperial]] forces [[Mission to Sulon|returned]] to the region and removed the tiles from the workshop. Kyle managed to sneak into the house and find him covered in plasters. Clearing the droid from dirt and dents in his processor housing, and charged the reserve power supply, Kyle aligned two half slots and inserted an encrypted [[Hologram disc/Legends|holodisc]] left by his father; WeeGee played back a message revealing the existence of the Map, which however had already been stolen. The holodisc also released the compartment containing the hidden [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] on the behalf of Kyle's father.<ref name="RA">''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]''</ref><ref name="RAAD" /><ref name="DF2">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]''</ref>

Right then, the droid was activated, and failed to recognize Kyle, as he thought the human was younger, still studying on Carida, and was ready to attack the stranger with a rock; Kyle ensured that it was him, ten [[standard year/Legends|years]] older than WeeGee remembered him, as he was out of circulation that long; the droid thought that he was reactivated in happier times, but after five [[standard minute/Legends|minutes]] he was filled up in with new information, including the death of Morgan, and that Kyle had defected to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]]. WeeGee then revealed about Rahn's visit and the work on the ceiling.<ref name="RA" /><ref name="RAAD" />

Together WeeGee and Kyle snuck out from the [[Katarn family compound|homestead]]'s kitchen, but misunderstanding Kyle's instructions, he instead exited by breaking through the door, splintering it. The two guards assigned there by the [[Imperial Remnant]], were shocked at first, allowing Kyle some time to shoot them with his [[blaster/Legends|blaster]].
[[File:Weegeekickingbutt.jpg|thumb|WeeGee battles [[8-13]] in the tunnels beneath the farm.]]
WeeGee led him to the hatch leading to the maintenance tunnels of the irrigation tunnel, providing also lighting. Somewhere in the tunnel, past the main central shaft and on their way to the vehicle park, they were attacked by [[Hunter killer droid|Hunter killer]] [[Battle droid/Legends|war droid]] [[8-13]]; despite heavily armed, WeeGee wrestled with it in close combat; WeeGee's non-humanoid configuration and lack of organs were an advantage until eventually decapitating his adversary with his power grasper.<ref name="RA" /><ref name="RAAD" />

Soon after they reached the hatch under the vehicle park, and it happened that they were right beneath an old [[T-4 (vehicle)|T-4]] transport. WeeGee momentarily had a conflict in his programming, considering that Morgan would not approve of Kyle stealing a vehicle.<ref name="RA" /><ref name="RAAD" /> Soon they were pursued by [[speeder bike/Legends|speeder bikes]], and WeeGee had to activate the second set of controls to drive the transport over a bridge while Kyle was fending a [[swoop gang/Legends|band]] of [[Grave Tuskens]] following them.<ref name="RA" />

WeeGee was unable to accelerate more and the vehicle was boarded. While Kyle was busy fighting the riders, he zoomed and noticed a roadblock set by the Imperials further ahead the bridge. Right then he saw an [[Moldy Crow/Legends|unknown ship]] above them. As told by Kyle, he set the speeder to auto, and rose towards the rear expecting for some "[[Jan Ors|Jan]]" to pick them up. He took a superficial damage at the side of his housing, which was retaliated by Kyle. The ship continued to lower and started to extend a ramp. Immediately he grabbed Kyle from his [[utlity belt/Legends|utlity belt]] and used his repulsors to lift both of them inside the ship. That was moments before the T-4 impacted the barricade resulting in an explosion that killed both Tuskens and Imperials.<ref name="RA" />

WeeGee was still in the ''Crow'' when Jan came to take him from the roof of the [[Government House (Sulon)|Government House]]. WeeGee noticed a shuttle leaving just then, which Kyle understood it was his target, [[8t88/Legends|8t88]]. Kyle asked where one would go to leave the planet, and the droid pointed out [[Fuel City]] as the nearest space port. After the ship dropped Kyle on station six of the Fuel City, the ''Crow'' departed according to orders, and WeeGee questioned Jan whether it was too dangerous for Kyle to be left by himself without support. Jan, who knew better how Kyle matured up, told him to get used to it. WeeGee remained with Jan as she returned to the Fuel City and was attacked by [[heat-seeking missile/Legends|heat-seeking missiles]], but managed to extract Kyle, whom WeeGee congratulated.<ref name="RAAD" />

[[File:WeeGee.jpg|thumb|left|WeeGee showing [[8t88/Legends|8t88's]] map to [[Valley of the Jedi]].]]
The pair returned to vicinity of the Katarn farm, and WeeGee was lookout while they rested during the night. The next day they returned to the homestead, and found equipment with which they connected WeeGee with 8t88's severed head, and successfully displayed a [[Holomap/Legends|holomap]], an electronic version of his father's Map. Even though Jerec had it already, the race to the Valley would continue.<ref name="RA" /><ref name="RAAD" /><ref name="DF2" />

WeeGee was taken by the pair back to the ''[[New Hope]]'' in the [[Milagro system/Legends|Milagro system]] where they were engaged in a [[battle in the Milagro system|battle]] and planned their next moves concerning Jerec and the Valley. WeeGee joined the strike force that utilized the captured [[transport/Legends|transport]] ''[[High Hauler]]'' all the way to Ruusan, in Jerec's [[Vengeance Battle Group|blockade]]. WeeGee was in the ''Moldy Crow'' when it was secretly dropped by the ''Hauler'' above Ruusan's [[atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]; as it flew among the clouds, WeeGee commented that the planet was very different from Sulon.<ref name="JK">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]''</ref>

The team eventually reached an [[unidentified Ruusan temple|ancient temple]] which was the hideout of the planet's colonists. WeeGee remained in the ''Crow'' to guard it while Kyle and Jan talked with them. A few days later the team travelled to a canyon near the [[Valley of the Jedi]], and Kyle insisted that they had to climb a mountain and WeeGee could not come with them, so he remained onboard. As it turned out, [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] was aware of their arrival and his men captured Jan and the ''Crow''. WeeGee was locked in a storage compartment, and the ship was docked with Jerec's ship, the ''[[Sulon Star]]''. Katarn however managed to reach the ship and heard Weeg's beeps. Things didn't go well because as Kyle eventually activated and released the ship and gained some distance from the crashing freighter, he couldn't gain height and the ''Crow'' crashed.<ref name="JK" /><ref name="DF2" />

[[File:Kylejanweegee.png|thumb|WeeGee with Kyle and Jan in the Valley.]]
The two survived the crash, but Kyle never had the time to release WeeGee, or even think about him, as he was pulled out by Jerec's [[Seven Dark Jedi|remaining servants]] to execute him. Kyle fought his way and reached the core of the Valley of the Jedi where he rescued Jan and confronted Jerec. In the meantime WeeGee also managed to escape the wreckage and followed Kyle's traces without him noticing. It was only after the death of Jerec when WeeGee approached Kyle.<ref name="JK" /> He and Jan stood near the self-taught Jedi as he created a monument to his father and Rahn.<ref name="JK" /><ref name="DF2" />

==Characteristics==
His extremely mild manners and lack of weapons were compensated by his heavy duty equipment and programming to protect [[Kyle Katarn]] and [[Katarn estate|their estate]] at all costs.<ref name="RA" /><ref name="RAAD" /> Probably not having been exposed to any [[Memory wipe/Legends|memory wipes]], the droid had developed a personality of its own.

He seemed to care about Kyle's moral upbringing and decisions, to the point that when Kyle considered stealing an Imperial vehicle for their escape, WeeGee brought up his father, apparently pointing out that he wouldn't approve. WeeGee was familiar with the controls of [[Harvester/Legends|combine]] and other farming equipment, which allowed him to pilot a T-4.<ref name="RA" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|Dad really did build droids to last!|Kyle Katarn|<ref name="RAAD" />}}
Weeg could rotate parts of his body in various configurations although commonly he took the shape of an inverted "U or V". He was equipped with an extremely powerful, four-jointed heavy-duty arm that ended with a C-shaped grasping appendage. It was three times stronger than the left arm, which had a [[human/Legends|Human]]-shaped [[limb/Legends|hand]] designed for using tools and other manipulations.<ref name="RA" />

WeeGee hovered two meters off the ground using a noisy [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] from an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] and steering jets from a [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droid]]. On top of the drive assembly was a vertical sensor stack that contained WeeGee's oval-shaped optical lens.<ref name="SFTE" /> He was equipped with [[Floodlight/Legends|Floodlights]] that illuminated a distance of about seventeen [[Meter/Legends|Imperial standard meters]].<ref name="RA" />

WeeGee communicated via beeps and whistles like a standard [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech]]-unit, which Kyle Katarn could understand, but also had a vocabulator which allowed him to speak in [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]; however when switched on, it drained some power from his servos.<ref name="RAAD" />

==Behind the scenes==
WeeGee was created for the video game ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' and appears in the cutscenes advancing the storyline. He also appears as as an in-game model, and it is possible to attack and destroy him by dealing sufficient damage, although the droid can withstand a lot. WeeGee explodes, instantly killing the player if he is close enough, and [[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]] says, "For you the choice is the dark side." The player also shifts closer to the dark side by about a twentieth of the morality scale meter. However, the player can be redeemed by choosing light side powers and avoiding civilian casualties. This is obviously non-canon, since WeeGee is shown later in the game and in other works.

WeeGee then appears in ''[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire]]'', taking place several years earlier, and then in ''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'', being the novelization of the game. The novella doesn't explain how WeeGee is reactivated, but the audio adaptation has Kyle charging up his reserve power. Both in the game and the novelization he speaks in binary, but in the novelization he beeps and whistles and some of his words are "translated" for the reader, as understood by Kyle Katarn. On the other hand, the [[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|audio adaptation]] mentions that WeeGee possesses a vocabulator, which Kyle activates, so that WeeGee's words can be heard and understood. In the audio, his voice is provided by [[Cliff Rakerd]].

In the novella, Katarn has to power up the T-4 and lift it up so that WeeGee can leave the hatch underneath; in the dramatization, he points out that he can leave thanks to his configuration, and boards the T-4 with Kyle.

After Kyle reunites with WeeGee, the game and the ''Rebel Agent'' novella/audio drama diverge greatly: the article follows the ''Rebel Agent'' version, where Kyle does not have the heart to leave the droid behind, because he considers WeeGee to be the only family he has left, and decides to bring him along and they organize together their escape; he then leaves him in Jan's custody. In the game, Kyle leaves WeeGee behind and escapes the house alone; instead of the underground tunnels and the vehicle chase, Kyle follows raised aqueducts/water bridges and battles creatures such as [[mailoc]]s before reaching Jan. It seems however that they later retrieve the droid, as WeeGee appears again when Kyle and Jan use him to decipher the data in 8t88's head. He also shows up in the end of the game, in the Valley of the Jedi, after [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec's]] defeat.
